name: filter0_basic
description: Filter 0 (no prediction) with shift=0 over a varied nibble pattern. Exercises the sign-extension trick and the >>1 step that produces the 15-bit-pre-wrap intermediate value.
provenance_notes: Header 0x00 = SSSS FFLE = 0000 0000 → shift=0, filter=0, no flags. Filter 0 means output is just the shifted nibble; no prediction terms. Per-sample: shifted = (sign_ext(nibble) << 0) >> 1 = sign_ext(nibble) >> 1 (arithmetic right shift). Walk: byte 1 = 0x12 → sample 0 high nibble 0x1 → sign-ext +1 → 1>>1 = 0; sample 1 low nibble 0x2 → +2 → 2>>1 = 1. byte 4 = 0x78 → sample 6 nibble 0x7 → +7 → 7>>1 = 3; sample 7 nibble 0x8 → -8 → -8>>1 = -4. byte 5 = 0x9A → sample 8 nibble 0x9 → -7 → -7>>1 = -4 (arithmetic shift floors negatives). 15-bit wrap is a no-op since every sample is in -8..+7. Sources: nocash fullsnes BRR section + boldowa/snesbrr decoder + SNESdev wiki. Expected_pcm cross-verified by core/fixtures/brr/_reference.py.
